symptoms.symptom_name: Crushing central chest pain radiating to left arm and jaw

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Classic cardiac referral pattern; one of the highest-yield historical features for ACS.

symptoms.symptom_name: Profuse diaphoresis with sense of impending doom

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Autonomic activation accompanying significant myocardial ischaemia.
May reflect early left ventricular dysfunction accompanying ischaemia.

symptoms.symptom_name: No prior angina history, no GTN available

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: First presentation of ACS is common; absence of prior angina does not reduce likelihood.
Suggests reduced left ventricular compliance consistent with acute ischaemia; clinician-only finding sealed from telehealth chat.

symptoms.symptom_name: Elevated high-sensitivity troponin and dynamic ECG ST changes

investigation_details.expected_result: ST-segment elevation or depression and/or elevated troponin confirming myocardial injury
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Diagnosis unconfirmed until paramedic/ED assessment; telehealth cannot obtain these directly.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Definitive diagnostic criteria per ACS guidelines.

symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Crushing chest pain with jaw/arm radiation, diaphoresis with impending doom, and resting dyspnoea together constitute the classic ACS tetrad mandating immediate emergency ambulance dispatch.

investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Identifies STEMI requiring immediate cath lab activation; cannot be performed via telehealth.
Required to confirm or exclude NSTEMI; unavailable via telehealth.
Not appropriate in the acute unstable setting; ECG plus troponin plus clinical assessment is the correct immediate pathway.
